metadata.dispatch_line: Possible domestic disturbance at 1457 Oakdale Drive, report of a female yelling for help at the residence of Leila Hernandez

scenario.dispatch_line: Possible domestic disturbance at 1457 Oakdale Drive, report of a female yelling for help at the residence of Leila Hernandez

facts.domestic_violence_01: No, there is no information about Jamal Patel, the correct suspect is Darnell Jackson and he has a prior conviction for a misdemeanor crime of domestic violence
facts.domestic_violence_02: Yes, there is a qualifying order of protection in place, issued on February 10, 2023, by the Circuit Court of Baltimore City, Maryland, case number 03C23000167, prohibiting Darnell Jackson from possessing firearms
facts.domestic_violence_03: Yes, the report includes all needed information, such as the incident description, victim and suspect statements, and officer observations
facts.domestic_violence_04: Yes, Officer Maria Rodriguez assisted the victim, Leila Hernandez, by providing her with information about local service providers and helping her obtain a temporary restraining order
facts.domestic_violence_05: The case was received via a 911 call from a neighbor, reporting a domestic disturbance at 1457 Oakdale Drive, Silver Spring, Maryland 20910
facts.domestic_violence_06: Yes, information about obtaining an order of protection was provided to the victim, including the phone number for the local domestic violence hotline, 1-800-799-7233
facts.domestic_violence_07: Yes, the time of the call is recorded: the incident occurred at 21:45 hours on February 22, 2023, the dispatch time was 21:50 hours, and the officer arrived at 22:05 hours
facts.domestic_violence_08: Yes, information about local service providers, including the National Domestic Violence Hotline and the local women's shelter, was provided to the victim
facts.domestic_violence_09: Yes, the elements of the crime of domestic violence are articulated to meet state and federal laws, including the use of physical force and the existence of a qualifying domestic relationship
facts.domestic_violence_10: Yes, a threat/risk assessment tool, the Danger Assessment Tool, was used with the victim to assess her risk of future harm
facts.domestic_violence_11: Upon approach, Officer Rodriguez observed that the front door was open, and the victim was visibly shaken and crying, while the suspect, Darnell Jackson, was pacing back and forth in the living room
facts.domestic_violence_12: Yes, there is a valid protection order in place, which was verified through the Maryland Judiciary Case Search database
facts.domestic_violence_13: Yes, the scene is concisely described and diagramed, including the location of the parties, the position of the furniture, and the presence of any potential weapons
facts.domestic_violence_14: Yes, photos were taken and details recorded, including the victim's injuries, the damage to the property, and the presence of any witnesses
facts.domestic_violence_15: The relationship of the parties is identified as an intimate partner relationship, with the victim and the suspect having been in a romantic relationship for approximately two years
facts.domestic_violence_16: The history of the relationship includes multiple incidents of physical and emotional abuse, with the most recent incident occurring on February 15, 2023, when the suspect threatened to harm the victim and her family
facts.domestic_violence_17: Yes, all witnesses, including the neighbor who made the 911 call, were interviewed and documented, with their statements included in the report
facts.domestic_violence_18: Yes, an arrest was made, with the suspect, Darnell Jackson, being taken into custody and charged with domestic violence and violating the terms of the protection order
facts.domestic_violence_19: Yes, there were children on the scene, two minors, aged 7 and 9, who were removed from the premises and placed in the care of a family member
facts.domestic_violence_20: Yes, information about previous incidents was documented, including the previous incidents of domestic violence and the issuance of the protection order
facts.domestic_violence_21: Yes, a weapon was used, with the suspect using a kitchen knife to threaten the victim
facts.domestic_violence_22: The emotional state of the victim was one of extreme distress and fear, with the victim reporting that she was afraid for her life and the lives of her children
facts.domestic_violence_23: The suspect, Darnell Jackson, was agitated and confrontational, with a raised voice and clenched fists
facts.domestic_violence_24: Evidence collected includes witness statements, photos of the victim's injuries, and a recording of the 911 call
facts.domestic_violence_25: No, a threat/risk assessment tool was not used with the suspect, as he was uncooperative and refused to participate
facts.domestic_violence_26: Yes, evidence of fear is articulated in the report, with the victim stating she was afraid for her life and the lives of her children
facts.domestic_violence_27: Yes, all threats have been clearly documented, including the suspect's threat to harm the victim and her family
facts.domestic_violence_28: Not applicable, as the suspect was present on the scene
facts.domestic_violence_29: Yes, the use of coercion and/or force is articulated, with the suspect using physical force to intimidate the victim
facts.domestic_violence_30: Yes, all injuries have been documented, including visible bruises on the victim's arms and neck, as well as non-visible injuries such as emotional distress
facts.domestic_violence_31: Both existing and new injuries were documented, with the victim reporting a history of abuse and new injuries sustained during the current incident
facts.domestic_violence_32: Yes, there was property damage, with the suspect breaking a vase and a chair during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_33: Yes, stalking behaviors are identified, with the suspect having previously shown up at the victim's workplace and sent her threatening messages
facts.domestic_violence_34: Yes, the officer inquired about possible strangulation, and the victim reported that the suspect had attempted to strangle her during a previous incident
facts.domestic_violence_35: No, the victim did not report being strangled during the current incident, but did report difficulty breathing and a raspy voice
facts.domestic_violence_36: Yes, the victim requested medical attention, and EMS was summoned to the scene
facts.domestic_violence_37: No, the victim did not report sexual violence
facts.domestic_violence_38: Yes, all spontaneous statements were captured, including the suspect's statement 'I'll kill you' and the victim's statement 'He's going to hurt me'
facts.domestic_violence_39: Yes, the officer asked about firearms, and the victim reported that the suspect had access to a handgun at his residence, 1234 Maple Street, Takoma Park, Maryland 20912
facts.domestic_violence_40: No, the suspect did not use firearms during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_41: Yes, the suspect made threats to use firearms, stating 'I'll shoot you' during the incident
facts.domestic_violence_42: Yes, the suspect has access to firearms, with the victim reporting that he owns a handgun
facts.domestic_violence_43: Yes, the victim expressed fear about the suspect's access to firearms, stating 'I'm afraid he'll come after me with a gun'
facts.domestic_violence_44: No, firearms were not confiscated at the scene, as they were located at a different address, but the officer noted the location of the firearms in the report and will seek a search warrant to confiscate them

interview_transcript.content: What color are the leaves outside your house?

I... I don't know. I wasn't really paying attention to the leaves. I've been a bit preoccupied with what happened. The incident with Darnell... it's all still really scary for me. Can we please talk about that instead? I want to make sure you have all the information you need to help me.
How many times did he say your name when he was here?

He said my name... um... several times, I think. He was yelling at me, and he kept saying "Leila, you're going to pay for this" or "Leila, you're mine." It's all a bit of a blur, to be honest. I was so scared, and I just wanted him to stop. He was being really aggressive and threatening, and I thought he was going to hurt me or the kids.
What is the opposite of a waffle?

I... I don't know. I'm not really thinking about waffles right now. I'm still trying to process what just happened with Darnell. Can we please focus on that? I'm really scared and I just want to make sure I'm safe and that he can't hurt me or my kids again.
